note: Mathlib/Data/Nat/Choose/Basic.lean:50 — `def choose : ℕ → ℕ → ℕ` is the binomial coefficient C(n,k), computed by the standard Pascal recursion.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at Basic.lean:50 (docstring: 'choose n k is the number of k-element subsets in an n-element set. Also known as binomial coefficient'); Wikidata Q209875 label/description = 'binomial coefficient' / 'family of positive integers that occur as coefficients in the binomial theorem' — exact is honest, not overclaimed.

note: Mathlib/GroupTheory/Finiteness.lean:395 — `class Group.FG : Prop` states the top subgroup is finitely generated (Group.fg_iff gives the closure-of-a-finite-set characterization); this IS the finitely-generated-group property.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at Finiteness.lean:395 (docstring: 'A group is finitely generated if it is finitely generated as a subgroup of itself'), matching Wikidata Q21083858 description verbatim in substance; exact is warranted.

note: Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Altitude.lean:236 — `height` = distance from a simplex vertex to the foot of its altitude; generalizes the elementary linear-measure height (mathworld/proofwiki sense) from triangles to n-simplices.
verify_note: Wrong QID target: Q208826's actual Wikidata label/description is 'height' / 'distance between the lowest end and highest end of an object' — the broad, overwhelmingly non-mathematical everyday concept (human/building/mountain height; enwiki sitelink 'Height' covers pitch, tides, tree data structures, etc., with geometric triangle-altitude as one throwaway subsection). Wikidata has a distinct, correct item for the geometric notion — Q339495 'Altitude' ('line segment in a triangle') — that the proposer should have targeted instead. Tagging a narrow n-simplex decl as a 'generalization' of the polysemous general-height concept overclaims relevance and misattributes the join to the wrong entity.

note: Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/UpperHalfPlane/Metric.lean:42 — defines the Poincaré (hyperbolic) distance on the upper half-plane, `dist z w = 2*arsinh(...)`; formalizes one specific model of hyperbolic geometry, not the general axiomatic theory (no synthetic/abstract hyperbolic-plane axiom system found).
verify_note: Decl confirmed at Metric.lean:42, file docstring explicitly says 'hyperbolic (Poincaré) distance'; Wikidata Q209306 = 'hyperbolic geometry' / 'type of non-Euclidean geometry' (unambiguous, unlike the Height case). special_case is an honest, non-overclaiming description of one model formalizing the general theory.

note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/Primorial.lean:40 — defines the primorial function n# itself (product of primes ≤ n); the derived notion 'primorial prime' (n#±1 prime) is NOT defined/stated anywhere in Mathlib, so this is only the underlying ingredient, not the concept.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at Primorial.lean:40; grepped the whole file and Mathlib for any n#±1 primality statement — none exists. Wikidata Q2085786 = 'primorial prime' / 'prime number that is product of first n primes ±1', confirming the concept is indeed distinct from and unformalized beyond the primorial function. 'related' + low confidence honestly flags the gap rather than overclaiming exact/special_case, so it survives review.
